messages.content: You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father's elder brother, my jethi buba, was the one who sold the land.
Note B: It was my father's younger brother who sold the land. He was always the reckless one.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "land sold by father's elder brother (jethi buba)", "note_b_claim": "land sold by father's younger brother", "resolution": "flag — elder versus younger brother, confirm paternal uncle's birth order and which uncle managed land decisions"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The man who taught me to read was a schoolteacher named Hari Sir.
Note B: My reading teacher was a retired government officer, not a trained teacher. Everyone called him Gopal dai.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "reading teacher was schoolteacher named Hari Sir", "note_b_claim": "reading teacher was retired government officer named Gopal dai", "resolution": "flag — different name and different profession for same role, confirm whether two different teachers or one person mis-remembered"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother's eldest sister, my thuli maijyu, raised me after my mother died.
Note B: After my mother passed, I was raised by my mother's youngest sister. She had no children of her own.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "raised by mother's eldest sister (thuli maijyu)", "note_b_claim": "raised by mother's youngest sister, childless", "resolution": "flag — eldest versus youngest maternal aunt as guardian, confirm which aunt was childless and which took in speaker"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The village headman during those years was a man named Dhan Bahadur.
Note B: Our mukhiya at that time was called Bir Singh. He was a tall man with a white mustache.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "village headman named Dhan Bahadur", "note_b_claim": "village headman named Bir Singh, described physically", "resolution": "flag — different names for same role in same period, confirm whether these were consecutive headmen or the same person with two names"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My husband's father was a farmer his whole life.
Note B: My father-in-law had worked as a soldier before settling on the land. He rarely spoke about his time in the army.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "father-in-law was farmer his whole life", "note_b_claim": "father-in-law was soldier before becoming farmer", "resolution": "flag — whole-life farmer versus prior military service, confirm with other family members whether army service preceded farming"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The woman everyone called Kanchi Buwa was actually the wife of the old carpenter.
Note B: Kanchi Buwa was the carpenter herself. Her husband died young and she took over his work.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "Kanchi Buwa was carpenter's wife", "note_b_claim": "Kanchi Buwa was the carpenter herself after husband's death", "resolution": "flag — same name, different role, confirm whether she worked as carpenter or only kept the household after husband died"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My cousin Raju was my father's brother's son.
Note B: Raju was actually my mother's side cousin, not my father's side. Our mothers were sisters.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "Raju is paternal cousin (father's brother's son)", "note_b_claim": "Raju is maternal cousin (mothers are sisters)", "resolution": "flag — paternal versus maternal lineage for same named cousin, confirm which parent's family Raju belongs to"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The priest at our family puja was always the same pandit, an old man from Bhaktapur.
Note B: Our family pandit was a young man from the valley, not very old at all. He had studied in Varanasi.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "family pandit was old man from Bhaktapur", "note_b_claim": "family pandit was young man, Varanasi-educated", "resolution": "flag — age and origin directly contradict, confirm whether two different priests served at different periods or one person is mis-remembered"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My second son Ram went to Kathmandu first, before any of his brothers.
Note B: It was my eldest who went to Kathmandu first. Ram only went later, after his elder brother had already found work there.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "second son Ram was first to go to Kathmandu", "note_b_claim": "eldest son went first, Ram followed later", "resolution": "flag — birth order and migration sequence conflict, confirm eldest son's name and who migrated first"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The doctor who delivered most of the children in our valley was called Dr. Sharma.
Note B: The only doctor in the valley was a man named Dr. Thapa. There was no one called Sharma in the medical line there.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "valley doctor named Dr. Sharma", "note_b_claim": "valley doctor named Dr. Thapa, Sharma name disputed", "resolution": "flag — surname contradiction for the same singular role, confirm doctor's surname, possibly two doctors in different eras"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My bhauju — my elder brother's wife — was from Sindhupalchok.
Note B: My bhauju came from a family in Dolakha. Her father was a trader on the old salt route.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "elder brother's wife from Sindhupalchok", "note_b_claim": "elder brother's wife from Dolakha, trader family", "resolution": "flag — two different districts of origin for same family member, confirm bhauju's birth district"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The man who bought our land was a moneylender from the district headquarters.
Note B: The land was sold to a farmer from the next village over, a decent man who needed more fields.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "land buyer was moneylender from district headquarters", "note_b_claim": "land buyer was local farmer from neighboring village", "resolution": "flag — profession and origin directly contradict, confirm identity of land purchaser, emotionally significant distinction"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father's name was Bal Bahadur.
Note B: My father was called Bir Bahadur. Bal Bahadur was his elder brother.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "father's name was Bal Bahadur", "note_b_claim": "father's name was Bir Bahadur, Bal Bahadur was uncle", "resolution": "flag — direct name swap between father and uncle, high-priority confirmation needed, similar names may have caused confusion across tellings"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The woman who helped during my first childbirth was a trained nurse from the health post.
Note B: There was no nurse when my first was born. An elderly neighbor woman, a traditional birth attendant, delivered my child.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "first childbirth assisted by trained nurse from health post", "note_b_claim": "first childbirth assisted by elderly neighbor, traditional birth attendant", "resolution": "flag — trained professional versus traditional attendant for same birth, confirm who was present at first delivery"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother-in-law had four sons and no daughters.
Note B: My sasu had three sons and two daughters. One daughter died young.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "mother-in-law had four sons, no daughters", "note_b_claim": "mother-in-law had three sons and two daughters, one died young", "resolution": "flag — total children and gender count both differ, confirm number of children and whether a daughter who died young was omitted from Note A"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The lama who performed the funeral rites was from a monastery in Solukhumbu.
Note B: The lama at the funeral came from the local gompa, just above the village. He walked down that morning.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "funeral lama came from Solukhumbu monastery, distant", "note_b_claim": "funeral lama came from local gompa above village, walked down same morning", "resolution": "flag — distant monastery versus local gompa for same funeral officiant, confirm lama's origin"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My eldest daughter Sunita married a man who worked in the carpet business.
Note B: Sunita's husband was a government teacher. He taught at the district school.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "Sunita's husband works in carpet business", "note_b_claim": "Sunita's husband is a government teacher", "resolution": "flag — profession directly contradicted for same son-in-law, confirm current and past occupation, possibly changed careers"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The sadhu who stayed under the pipal tree every Shivaratri came from somewhere in India, no one knew exactly where.
Note B: That sadhu was a local man from three villages away. People recognized him. He just dressed like a sadhu for the festival.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "sadhu was from India, origin unknown", "note_b_claim": "sadhu was local man from nearby village, recognized by community", "resolution": "flag — foreign ascetic versus known local person in ritual disguise, confirm whether the person was genuinely recognized or rumored to be local"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My grandfather on my father's side never went to school. He could not read.
Note B: My paternal grandfather was educated in a Sanskrit school. He could read and write in Devanagari and he kept a small diary.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "paternal grandfather illiterate, no schooling", "note_b_claim": "paternal grandfather attended Sanskrit school, literate, kept diary", "resolution": "flag — literacy status directly contradicted for same person, confirm with any surviving documents or family members who knew him"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My mother was the youngest of five sisters.
Note B: My mother was the middle child — she had two elder sisters and two younger ones. Five children total.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "mother was youngest of five sisters", "note_b_claim": "mother was middle child of five, two younger sisters below her", "resolution": "flag — youngest versus middle birth position among same number of siblings, confirm mother's birth order and siblings' ages"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: The man who ran the tea shop near the bus stop was called Harka.
Note B: The tea shop owner was named Dil Bahadur. Harka was his son who sometimes helped behind the counter.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "tea shop owner named Harka", "note_b_claim": "tea shop owner named Dil Bahadur, Harka was his son and helper", "resolution": "flag — name applied to owner versus son for same shop, confirm which family member ran the counter, Harka may have been more visible"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My father's first wife — before my mother — died in childbirth.
Note B: My father was never married before my mother. She was his first and only wife.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "father had a first wife who died in childbirth before current mother", "note_b_claim": "mother was father's first and only wife, no prior marriage", "resolution": "flag — existence of prior marriage directly disputed, sensitive family history, confirm carefully with elder relatives"}

Note A: The person who donated the land for the school was a wealthy merchant from our village.
Note B: The school land was donated by a man who came from outside, a government official who had grown up nearby.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "school land donor was local wealthy merchant", "note_b_claim": "school land donor was outside government official with local roots", "resolution": "flag — insider merchant versus outside official for same donation, confirm donor's profession and village of origin"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My buba — my grandfather — came back from the war missing two fingers on his left hand.
Note B: My grandfather had all his fingers. It was his brother who lost fingers in the fighting.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "grandfather missing two fingers on left hand from war", "note_b_claim": "grandfather had all fingers, injury belonged to his brother", "resolution": "flag — distinctive physical detail transferred between grandfather and great-uncle, confirm which relative had the hand injury"}

You are a reconciler agent. Given two memory notes, detect if they describe the same person with contradictory names or roles. Output JSON with keys: conflict, conflict_type, note_a_claim, note_b_claim, resolution. Output only valid JSON, nothing else.

Note A: My husband's youngest brother was the one who never married and stayed home to care for the parents.
Note B: It was my husband's eldest brother who remained unmarried and looked after the old people. The youngest moved to Pokhara.

{"conflict": true, "conflict_type": "person identity conflict", "note_a_claim": "youngest brother-in-law stayed home unmarried as caretaker", "note_b_claim": "eldest brother-in-law stayed home unmarried, youngest moved to Pokhara", "resolution": "flag — caretaker role and city migration assigned to opposite brothers, confirm birth order and residence of each brother-in-law"}
